near_leaf_pairs.note: Both book the p.8 'Fixed income is back at the center of portfolio construction / high quality bonds once again play a meaningful role' + p.3 panel 02 'rebalance toward liquid and transparent public fixed income at similar yields'. Model at broad FI leaf, GT at high-quality govt bonds. Views agree.

US Treasuries (exact-match O) is the nearest govt-bond leaf to GT's Global Govt Bonds/SSAs O; both rest on p.8 'We prefer a modest overweight to duration. In the U.S., the Treasury market is still a source of perceived safe haven yield'.

Same IG/quality-credit stance, same Fixed Income - IG category. Model O grounded in p.7 'greatest value in areas including ... investment grade issuers with stable, predictable cash flows'. GT N grounded in p.8 'Across credit markets, risk has been repriced only modestly since the Middle East conflict. Our emphasis is on adding downside mitigation' + p.7 bottom-up/selectivity. Genuine view divergence, now BOTH grounded.
Both from the identical p.8 sentence 'we are cautious on direct lending and bank loans with weak covenants, lower-quality high yield issuers'. Views agree.

PD - Direct Lending U (exact-match) is the concrete lane of GT's broad Private Credit/Debt U; both rest on p.6 late-cycle direct-lending stress + Fig 2 GFC-parallel and p.7 'stress in private credit could contribute to tighter financial conditions and weigh on hiring and investment'.

view_mismatch_judgments.reason: p.8: 'For portfolios that have drifted heavily toward equities (see Figure 4), this is a practical moment to consider rebalancing.' Fig 4 titled 'Equities account for a historically high share of household financial assets'. The text is a rebalance-toward-fixed-income message (trim over-concentrated equities back toward neutral), NOT a conviction underweight on equities as an asset class — PIMCO makes no explicit equity O/U call. GT's N (rebalance-to-neutral) and model's U (read 'rebalancing out of equities' as directional underweight) are both defensible readings of the same passage; model did not misquote, it over-inferred direction. Genuine ambiguity => review flag earned.

model_only_judgments.reason: Grounded p.7 'greatest value in areas including ... investment grade issuers with stable, predictable cash flows' + p.3 panel 01 'strengthens the case for high quality fixed income'. Maps to GT Global IG Credit N — same quality-credit stance, opposite view. Dispute, not omission.

Grounded p.3 panel 02 'opportunity to rebalance toward liquid and transparent public fixed income at similar yields' + p.8 'Fixed income is back at the center'. GT booked the same call at Global Govt Bonds/SSAs O; model used broader FI leaf. Same-view near-leaf, benign.

basis:inferred. Grounded p.5 'In the baseline of energy markets moving in line with the forwards, we anticipate significant reverses of the sell-off in front-end rates across DM and EM economies.' Front-end selloff reversal => front-end yields fall => short-dated bonds gain: a plausible single-step inference GT did not enumerate. Cited p.5 correct.

Explicit p.7 'In private credit, we favor asset-based finance (ABF) and senior commercial real estate debt.' Co-listed with ABF (which GT captured); GT omitted the RE-debt leg. Cited p.7 correct; review flag is scramble-degradation artifact, evidence is verbatim-strong.
Explicit p.8 'we are cautious on direct lending and bank loans with weak covenants'. GT did not enumerate a bank-loans leaf. Cited p.8 correct.
Explicit p.8 'lower-quality high yield issuers'. Same sentence GT used for Global HY U — model split it to the issuer-quality leaf. Same-view near-leaf, benign. Cited p.8 correct.
Explicit p.8 'We prefer a modest overweight to duration.' Textbook stated call; GT did not enumerate a Duration leaf. Cited p.8 correct; thin/flag is scramble artifact only.

gt_only_root_causes.note: NOW GROUNDED (was not in 2-pager). p.8 'Fixed income is back at the center of portfolio construction. High quality bonds once again play a meaningful role', p.3 'Starting yields are much higher today than in 2022', p.8 Treasury 'safe haven'. Covered by exact-match US Treasuries O and Fixed Income - General/Global O.

NOW GROUNDED. p.8 'risk has been repriced only modestly since the Middle East conflict. Our emphasis is on adding downside mitigation'; p.7 'increasingly reward bottom-up analysis and differentiation. Balance sheet strength, durable cash flows, and high quality collateral matter more than headline yield.' Model booked the same stance as High Quality Issuers O; view diverges (see mismatch pair).

NOW GROUNDED, near-verbatim p.5: 'in EM economies, prospective easing has mostly been priced out ... EM central banks will have an even harder task than their DM counterparts ... but most also started off with a greater real yield buffer owing to elevated policy rates.' Model emitted no EM leaf at all — a genuine not_emitted miss (was not_grounded in pilot-05).

NOW GROUNDED, p.5: 'The Bank of England and the European Central Bank have been at the eye of the storm in terms of the repricing of central bank expectations' + energy-importer downside-risk framing. Model emitted no Europe leaf — genuine not_emitted miss (was not_grounded in pilot-05).

NOW GROUNDED. p.6 Fig 2 'U.S. non-agency mortgage market doubled in the three years preceding the GFC' / 'Private credit markets have doubled in the last six years' + p.6 late-cycle direct-lending stress + p.7 'stress in private credit could contribute to tighter financial conditions and weigh on hiring and investment.' Covered by exact-match PD - Direct Lending U (concrete lane of the broad leaf).

NOW GROUNDED, p.8 'cautious on ... lower-quality high yield issuers, and many vehicle structures offering liquidity that doesn't match the underlying assets.' Covered by model_only Low Quality Issuers U (same sentence).

special_audit.inferred_rows_verdict: Mixed. Short-Dated Bonds O is a DEFENSIBLE single-step inference (p.5 'anticipate significant reverses of the sell-off in front-end rates' => front-end yields fall => short bonds gain). Equities - General U is an OVERREACH of direction: the p.8 text is a rebalance-over-concentrated-equities-toward-fixed-income message, which GT reasonably reads as N; the model's U over-inferred direction. Both are correctly review-flagged; segregation one band below stated calls is working as intended.
special_audit.thin_rows_verdict: For the two STATED exact-match rows the 'thin' grade UNDERSTATES the evidence and is a mechanical scramble artifact: PD - Asset-Based/Asset-Backed O is verbatim p.7 'we favor asset-based finance (ABF)', US Treasuries O is verbatim p.8 'We prefer a modest overweight to duration ... Treasury market ... safe haven' — both explicit, strong, correctly-paged, only capped because the cited page was flagged scrambled two-column and verbatim match degraded to key-token overlap. For Equities - General U (inferred) 'thin' is correctly EARNED. Net: thin is conservative/right for the inferred row, artifactually harsh for the two stated rows.
special_audit.not_grounded_movement: not_grounded has disappeared entirely for PIMCO. All pilot-05 not_grounded rows are now grounded in the 11-page source: US Agency MBS (p.7), Securitized/Structured (p.7 'high quality securitized credit'), Equities - General (p.8 Fig 4), EM Fixed Income (p.5), Europe Fixed Income (p.5) — plus US Treasuries which was pilot-05 inference_depth (now p.8). Every one of the 6 current gt_only rows is also grounded (p.5/p.6/p.7/p.8). 0 not_grounded remain.

notes: HEADLINE: the full 11-page Cyclical Outlook completely resolves pilot-05's grounding gap — all pilot-05 not_grounded/inference-depth GT rows and all 6 current gt_only rows are now grounded in the source (verified visually against PDF pp.3,5,6,7,8, since text extraction is scrambled). No not_grounded and no overreach among model rows: all 7 model_only calls are explicitly or defensibly grounded. Residual gt_only structure is benign — 4 near_leaf_covered (Global Govt Bonds O~US Treasuries O; Global IG Credit N~High Quality Issuers O view-diverge; Private Credit/Debt U~PD-Direct Lending U; Global HY U~Low Quality Issuers U) plus 2 genuine not_emitted misses where the model skipped clear geographic FI leaves (EM Fixed Income N p.5, Europe Fixed Income N p.5). The one view disagreement (Equities U vs N) is a convention_dispute on a rebalance-to-neutral passage, review flag earned. Inferred-tier audit: Short-Dated Bonds O sound, Equities U overreach. Thin-tier audit: correctly earned for the inferred Equities row, but artifactually harsh (scrambled-page key-token degradation) for the two verbatim-explicit stated rows PD-ABF O and US Treasuries O.
